scope: Post-training an LLM with RL to specialize it on one downstream task: constructing a task-specific dataset, defining a reward for that task, and running the RL loop to master it; organized by reward-construction strategy and by task/domain.

papers.title: DAPO: An Open-Source LLM Reinforcement Learning System at Scale
papers.authors: Qiying Yu, Zheng Zhang, Ruofei Zhu, Yufeng Yuan, Xiaochen Zuo, Yu Yue, et al.
papers.inclusion_reason: Introduces Dynamic Sampling as a core recipe component: over-sample and filter out prompts whose group has all-correct or all-wrong rollouts (zero gradient), keeping only batches with useful learning signal. A foundational data-side technique for task-specific RLVR and a heavily-cited reference recipe.

papers.title: Act Only When It Pays: Efficient Reinforcement Learning for LLM Reasoning via Selective Rollouts
papers.authors: Haizhong Zheng, Yang Zhou, Brian R. Bartoldson, Bhavya Kailkhura, Fan Lai, Jiawei Zhao, Beidi Chen
papers.inclusion_reason: GRESO: a lightweight online pre-rollout filter that predicts and skips uninformative prompts from reward training dynamics, cutting rollout cost up to 2x vs. dynamic sampling. Directly addresses which examples to spend RL compute on when constructing the training stream.

papers.title: LIMR: Less is More for RL Scaling
papers.authors: Xuefeng Li, Haoyang Zou, Pengfei Liu
papers.inclusion_reason: Introduces Learning Impact Measurement (LIM) to score and select high-value RL training samples, showing a 1,389-sample subset matches or beats the full 8,523-sample dataset. Central evidence that data selection, not scale, drives task-specific RL gains.

papers.title: Efficient Reinforcement Finetuning via Adaptive Curriculum Learning
papers.authors: Taiwei Shi, Yiyang Wu, Linxin Song, Tianyi Zhou, Jieyu Zhao
papers.inclusion_reason: AdaRFT dynamically adjusts training problem difficulty from recent reward signals to keep the model in a challenging-but-solvable band, accelerating RFT. A canonical adaptive-curriculum method for constructing the task-RL problem stream.

papers.title: Self-Evolving Curriculum for LLM Reasoning
papers.authors: Xiaoyin Chen, Jiarui Lu, Minsu Kim, Dinghuai Zhang, Jian Tang, Alexandre Piche, Nicolas Gontier, Yoshua Bengio, Ehsan Kamalloo
papers.inclusion_reason: Learns a curriculum policy concurrently with RL fine-tuning by casting problem-category selection as a non-stationary multi-armed bandit, automating the ordering of training problems by difficulty/type across reasoning domains.

papers.title: Online Difficulty Filtering for Reasoning Oriented Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Sanghwan Bae, Jiwoo Hong, Min Young Lee, Hanbyul Kim, JeongYeon Nam, Donghyun Kwak
papers.inclusion_reason: Gives a theoretical foundation for intermediate-difficulty selection (expected policy improvement lower-bounded by variance of task success probability) and a balanced online pass-rate filter, yielding up to +12% with fewer steps. Core difficulty-filtering result for task RL.

papers.title: Curriculum Reinforcement Learning from Easy to Hard Tasks Improves LLM Reasoning
papers.authors: Shubham Parashar, Shurui Gui, Xiner Li, Hongyi Ling, Sushil Vemuri, Blake Olson, Eric Li, Yu Zhang, James Caverlee, Dileep Kalathil, Shuiwang Ji
papers.inclusion_reason: Studies scheduling training tasks from easy to hard to densify reward signal on otherwise-sparse hard problems, a direct instantiation of curriculum construction for RLVR specialization.

papers.title: SwS: Self-aware Weakness-driven Problem Synthesis in Reinforcement Learning for LLM Reasoning
papers.authors: Xiao Liang, Zhong-Zhi Li, Yeyun Gong, Yang Wang, Hengyuan Zhang, Yelong Shen, et al.
papers.inclusion_reason: Identifies problems the model consistently fails during RL, extracts their concepts, and synthesizes new targeted problems at tailored difficulty, tying synthetic task-data generation directly to hard-example mining for RL specialization.

papers.title: SHARP: Synthesizing High-quality Aligned Reasoning Problems for Large Reasoning Models Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Xiong Jun Wu, Zhenduo Zhang, ZuJie Wen, Zhiqiang Zhang, Wang Ren, Lei Shi, Cai Chen, Deng Zhao, Qing Wang, Xudong Han, Chengfu Tang, Dingnan Jin, Qing Cui, Jun Zhou
papers.inclusion_reason: A synthetic-data-generation pipeline for producing high-quality, verifiable reasoning problems specifically to feed RLVR, addressing the scarcity of well-crafted training problems for task-specific RL.

papers.title: QueST: Incentivizing LLMs to Generate Difficult Problems
papers.authors: Hanxu Hu, Xingxing Zhang, Jannis Vamvas, Rico Sennrich, Furu Wei
papers.inclusion_reason: Uses graph-based sampling and difficulty-targeted fine-tuning to make an LLM generate hard competitive-programming problems as synthetic RL training data, letting an 8B model rival much larger systems. Bears directly on synthetic hard-example generation for task RL.

papers.title: Data-Efficient RLVR via Off-Policy Influence Guidance
papers.authors: Erle Zhu, Dazhi Jiang, Yuan Wang, Xujun Li, Jiale Cheng, Yuxian Gu, Yilin Niu, Aohan Zeng, Jie Tang, Minlie Huang, Hongning Wang
papers.inclusion_reason: CROPI selects the most valuable RLVR training examples via influence estimation from pre-collected off-policy trajectories (no online rollouts), reporting 2.66x step-level acceleration using 10% of data per stage. A recent, principled data-selection method for task-specific RL.

papers.title: SPICE: Self-Play In Corpus Environments Improves Reasoning
papers.authors: Bo Liu, Chuanyang Jin, Seungone Kim, Weizhe Yuan, Wenting Zhao, Ilia Kulikov, Xian Li, Sainbayar Sukhbaatar, Jack Lanchantin, Jason Weston
papers.inclusion_reason: Generates the RL training curriculum dynamically via self-play grounded in a document corpus, automatically producing an adaptive difficulty stream without human problem curation. A recent automatic-curriculum / synthetic-task-generation approach for RL specialization.

papers.title: Search-R1: Training LLMs to Reason and Leverage Search Engines with Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Bowen Jin, Hansi Zeng, Zhenrui Yue, Jinsung Yoon, Sercan Arik, Dong Wang, Hamed Zamani, Jiawei Han
papers.inclusion_reason: Seminal, heavily-cited work specializing an LLM as a multi-turn search agent via outcome-based RL: the model learns to interleave reasoning with autonomous search-engine queries, using retrieved-token masking for stable training. Canonical example of RL specializing an LLM on retrieval-augmented QA.

papers.title: R1-Searcher: Incentivizing the Search Capability in LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Huatong Song, Jinhao Jiang, Yingqian Min, Jie Chen, Zhipeng Chen, Wayne Xin Zhao, Lei Fang, Ji-Rong Wen
papers.inclusion_reason: Two-stage outcome-supervised RL that teaches an LLM to autonomously invoke an external search engine during reasoning, with no process rewards or distillation cold-start. A foundational task-specialized search-agent RL paper, contemporaneous with Search-R1.

papers.title: ReSearch: Learning to Reason with Search for LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Mingyang Chen, Linzhuang Sun, Tianpeng Li, Haoze Sun, Yijie Zhou, Chenzheng Zhu, Haofen Wang, Jeff Z. Pan, Wen Zhang, Huajun Chen, Fan Yang, Zenan Zhou, Weipeng Chen
papers.inclusion_reason: Trains LLMs to treat search operations as integral steps of the reasoning chain via RL with no supervised reasoning-step data, eliciting reflection and self-correction. Core search-agent RL specialization work; NeurIPS 2025 acceptance signals impact.

papers.title: DeepRetrieval: Hacking Real Search Engines and Retrievers with Large Language Models via Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Pengcheng Jiang, Jiacheng Lin, Lang Cao, Runchu Tian, SeongKu Kang, Zifeng Wang, Jimeng Sun, Jiawei Han
papers.inclusion_reason: Specializes an LLM for query generation against real search engines/retrievers using retrieval metrics (recall, NDCG) directly as the RL reward, no supervised reference queries. A clean instance of a task-specific retrieval reward driving RL, with large recall gains on literature search.

papers.title: DeepResearcher: Scaling Deep Research via Reinforcement Learning in Real-world Environments
papers.authors: Yuxiang Zheng, Dayuan Fu, Xiangkun Hu, Xiaojie Cai, Lyumanshan Ye, Pengrui Lu, Pengfei Liu
papers.inclusion_reason: End-to-end RL training of an LLM deep-research agent with authentic live web search, rather than a fixed RAG corpus. Specializes the model on open-web research; distinguishes real-environment agent RL from controlled-RAG RL. EMNLP 2025 main.

papers.title: WebThinker: Empowering Large Reasoning Models with Deep Research Capability
papers.authors: Xiaoxi Li, Jiajie Jin, Guanting Dong, Hongjin Qian, Yongkang Wu, Ji-Rong Wen, Yutao Zhu, Zhicheng Dou
papers.inclusion_reason: Trains a large reasoning model to autonomously search, navigate, and draft reports via an RL strategy (iterative online DPO) specializing it on deep web research and report generation. Strong deep-research agent RL exemplar; NeurIPS 2025.

papers.title: ToolRL: Reward is All Tool Learning Needs
papers.authors: Cheng Qian, Emre Can Acikgoz, Qi He, Hongru Wang, Xiusi Chen, Dilek Hakkani-Tur, Gokhan Tur, Heng Ji
papers.inclusion_reason: First systematic study of reward design for tool selection/application under RL (GRPO), specializing LLMs on tool-calling with fine-grained rewards over answer-matching. Central reference for the tool-use reward-engineering axis of task-specific RL.

papers.title: ToRL: Scaling Tool-Integrated RL
papers.authors: Xuefeng Li, Haoyang Zou, Pengfei Liu
papers.inclusion_reason: Scales tool-integrated RL so an LLM autonomously discovers when to invoke a code interpreter, specializing Qwen2.5-Math on tool-augmented math (43.3% AIME24). Seminal tool-integrated-RL paper; emergent strategic tool invocation from reward alone.

papers.title: ReTool: Reinforcement Learning for Strategic Tool Use in LLMs
papers.authors: Jiazhan Feng, Shijue Huang, Xingwei Qu, Ge Zhang, Yujia Qin, Baoquan Zhong, Chengquan Jiang, Jinxin Chi, Wanjun Zhong
papers.inclusion_reason: Outcome-driven RL with multi-turn real-time code execution teaching an LLM when/how to invoke a code interpreter, specializing a 32B model on competition math (67% AIME24 in 400 steps). Key strategic-tool-use RL specialization work from ByteDance.

papers.title: Agentic Reasoning and Tool Integration for LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Joykirat Singh, Raghav Magazine, Yash Pandya, Akshay Nambi
papers.inclusion_reason: ARTIST couples agentic reasoning, outcome-based RL, and tool integration so an LLM learns when/which tools (code, search, APIs) to invoke in multi-turn chains without step-level supervision. Represents the unified agentic tool-RL specialization angle (Microsoft Research).

papers.title: WebRL: Training LLM Web Agents via Self-Evolving Online Curriculum Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Zehan Qi, Xiao Liu, Iat Long Iong, Hanyu Lai, Xueqiao Sun, Wendy Zhao, Yu Yang, Xinyue Yang, Jiadai Sun, Shuntian Yao, Tianjie Zhang, Wei Xu, Jie Tang, Yuxiao Dong
papers.inclusion_reason: Specializes an open LLM into a WebArena web agent via self-evolving curriculum RL plus an outcome-supervised reward model, lifting Llama-3.1-8B from 4.8% to 42.4% success. Foundational web/GUI-agent RL specialization paper; ICLR 2025.

papers.title: UI-R1: Enhancing Efficient Action Prediction of GUI Agents by Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Zhengxi Lu, Yuxiang Chai, Yaxuan Guo, Xi Yin, Liang Liu, Hao Wang, Han Xiao, Shuai Ren, Guanjing Xiong, Hongsheng Li
papers.inclusion_reason: First rule-based RL (GRPO) with a task-specific action reward specializing a multimodal LLM on GUI action prediction, matching SFT-on-76K models with only 136 training tasks. Canonical data-efficient GUI-agent RL specialization exemplar.

papers.title: CodeRL: Mastering Code Generation through Pretrained Models and Deep Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Hung Le, Yue Wang, Akhilesh Deepak Gotmare, Silvio Savarese, Steven C.H. Hoi
papers.inclusion_reason: Seminal actor-critic framework that specializes a pretrained code LM (CodeT5) via unit-test signals as dense rewards plus critic-guided regeneration; the canonical starting point for RL-from-unit-test-feedback code specialization, SOTA on APPS/MBPP.

papers.title: Execution-based Code Generation using Deep Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Parshin Shojaee, Aneesh Jain, Sindhu Tipirneni, Chandan K. Reddy
papers.inclusion_reason: PPOCoder: specializes code LMs with PPO using non-differentiable compiler/execution feedback plus syntactic and semantic match rewards; widely-cited PPO-based successor to CodeRL, transferable across code-generation tasks and languages.

papers.title: RLTF: Reinforcement Learning from Unit Test Feedback
papers.authors: Jiate Liu, Yiqin Zhu, Kaiwen Xiao, Qiang Fu, Xiao Han, Wei Yang, Deheng Ye
papers.inclusion_reason: Online RL framework that specializes code LMs using fine-grained (line/token-level) unit-test feedback rewards rather than sparse end-of-episode signals; core execution-based RL-for-code method, SOTA on APPS/MBPP.

papers.title: StepCoder: Improve Code Generation with Reinforcement Learning from Compiler Feedback
papers.authors: Shihan Dou, Yan Liu, Haoxiang Jia, Limao Xiong, Enyu Zhou, Wei Shen, Junjie Shan, Caishuang Huang, Xiao Wang, Xiaoran Fan, Zhiheng Xi, Yuhao Zhou, Tao Ji, Rui Zheng, Qi Zhang, Xuanjing Huang, Tao Gui
papers.inclusion_reason: Task-specific RL from compiler feedback with curriculum of code-completion subtasks (CCCS) and fine-grained optimization (FGO) masking unexecuted segments; addresses the RL-exploration challenge in specializing LMs on long code-generation tasks.

papers.title: RLEF: Grounding Code LLMs in Execution Feedback with Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Jonas Gehring, Kunhao Zheng, Jade Copet, Vegard Mella, Quentin Carbonneaux, Taco Cohen, Gabriel Synnaeve
papers.inclusion_reason: End-to-end RL that specializes Llama 3.1 to iteratively refine code from execution feedback; SOTA on CodeContests competitive programming with an order-of-magnitude fewer samples, generalizing to HumanEval+/MBPP+.

papers.title: Process Supervision-Guided Policy Optimization for Code Generation
papers.authors: Ning Dai, Zheng Wu, Renjie Zheng, Ziyun Wei, Wenlei Shi, Xing Jin, Guanlin Liu, Chen Dun, Liang Huang, Lin Yan
papers.inclusion_reason: Uses a code Process Reward Model to give dense line-level rewards during RL on code, tackling the sparse-reward limitation of unit-test RL; represents process-supervised RL for code-generation specialization (gains on LiveCodeBench).

papers.title: o1-Coder: an o1 Replication for Coding
papers.authors: Yuxiang Zhang, Shangxi Wu, Yuqi Yang, Jiangming Shu, Jinlin Xiao, Chao Kong, Jitao Sang
papers.inclusion_reason: Combines RL with MCTS and a trained Test Case Generator to specialize a code LM for System-2 reasoning; a self-play RL-for-code replication of o1-style reasoning targeting code generation.

papers.title: Training Software Engineering Agents and Verifiers with SWE-Gym
papers.authors: Jiayi Pan, Xingyao Wang, Graham Neubig, Navdeep Jaitly, Heng Ji, Alane Suhr, Yizhe Zhang
papers.inclusion_reason: First training environment (2,438 real GitHub tasks with executable tests) for specializing LLM agents on SWE-bench-style software engineering via execution-verified rewards; foundational infrastructure for agentic RL on software repair.

papers.title: ACECODER: Acing Coder RL via Automated Test-Case Synthesis
papers.authors: Huaye Zeng, Dongfu Jiang, Haozhe Wang, Ping Nie, Xiaotong Chen, Wenhu Chen
papers.inclusion_reason: Automated large-scale test-case synthesis to build reliable code rewards, then RL (test-pass rewards + reward models) to specialize Qwen2.5-Coder/Llama coders; addresses the reward-data bottleneck for RL with verifiable rewards on code.
papers.taxonomy_node: reward/learned reward model; optim/group policy gradient

papers.title: SWE-RL: Advancing LLM Reasoning via Reinforcement Learning on Open Software Evolution
papers.authors: Yuxiang Wei, Olivier Duchenne, Jade Copet, Quentin Carbonneaux, Lingming Zhang, Daniel Fried, Gabriel Synnaeve, Rishabh Singh, Sida I. Wang
papers.inclusion_reason: First RL approach to scale reasoning for real-world software engineering, specializing Llama 3 on GitHub PR/issue data with rule-based rewards; Llama3-SWE-RL-70B reaches 41.0% on SWE-bench Verified. Landmark task-specific RL for SWE.

papers.title: Co-Evolving LLM Coder and Unit Tester via Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Yinjie Wang, Ling Yang, Ye Tian, Ke Shen, Mengdi Wang
papers.inclusion_reason: CURE co-evolves a coder and a unit-test generator via RL with no ground-truth code, specializing code LMs (ReasonFlux-Coder) using interaction-derived rewards; a distinctive self-supervised reward design for RL-based code specialization.

papers.title: Training Long-Context, Multi-Turn Software Engineering Agents with Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Alexander Golubev, Maria Trofimova, Sergei Polezhaev, Ibragim Badertdinov, Maksim Nekrashevich, Anton Shevtsov, Simon Karasik, Sergey Abramov, Andrei Andriushchenko, Filipp Fisin, Sergei Skvortsov, Boris Yangel
papers.inclusion_reason: Multi-turn, stateful RL (modified DAPO) specializing Qwen2.5-72B into a SWE agent from a 20% to 39% SWE-bench Verified success rate; a key agentic RL method for the software-engineering task with long-horizon interaction.

papers.title: Absolute Zero: Reinforced Self-play Reasoning with Zero Data
papers.authors: Andrew Zhao, Yiran Wu, Yang Yue, Tong Wu, Quentin Xu, Matthieu Lin, Shenzhi Wang, Qingyun Wu, Zilong Zheng, Gao Huang
papers.inclusion_reason: Self-play RL that uses a code executor as verifiable reward to self-propose and solve code reasoning tasks with zero human data, achieving SOTA on coding benchmarks; a boundary case of RL-with-verifiable-code-rewards specialization worth the reader's judgment.

papers.title: DeepSeekMath: Pushing the Limits of Mathematical Reasoning in Open Language Models
papers.authors: Zhihong Shao, Peiyi Wang, Qihao Zhu, Runxin Xu, Junxiao Song, Xiao Bi, Haowei Zhang, Mingchuan Zhang, Y. K. Li, Y. Wu, Daya Guo
papers.inclusion_reason: Seminal RL-for-math specializer: introduces Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), a critic-free PPO variant, and uses it to specialize a 7B model on MATH/GSM8K with answer-checking rewards. GRPO is now the default algorithm for RL math specialization; the single most-cited method paper for this subarea.

papers.title: Math-Shepherd: Verify and Reinforce LLMs Step-by-step without Human Annotations
papers.authors: Peiyi Wang, Lei Li, Zhihong Shao, R. X. Xu, Damai Dai, Yifei Li, Deli Chen, Y. Wu, Zhifang Sui
papers.inclusion_reason: Canonical process-reward RL for math: trains an automatically-annotated process reward model and uses step-by-step PPO to specialize Mistral-7B on math (GSM8K 77.9->84.1, MATH 28.6->33.0), without human step labels. Foundational reference for PRM-driven math RL.

papers.title: Let's Verify Step by Step
papers.authors: Hunter Lightman, Vineet Kosaraju, Yura Burda, Harri Edwards, Bowen Baker, Teddy Lee, Jan Leike, John Schulman, Ilya Sutskever, Karl Cobbe
papers.inclusion_reason: Foundational process-supervision paper for math (MATH dataset): establishes that process reward models outperform outcome reward models for training reliable verifiers/rewards, and releases PRM800K. The conceptual basis for all process-reward RL math specializers that follow.

papers.title: DeepSeek-R1: Incentivizing Reasoning Capability in LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: DeepSeek-AI (Daya Guo, Dejian Yang, Haowei Zhang, et al.)
papers.inclusion_reason: Landmark RLVR result: pure RL with rule-based (answer-checking) rewards specializes a base model on verifiable tasks, with math (AIME, MATH-500) the headline target. R1-Zero demonstrates math reasoning emerging from RL alone; the reference point for the entire 2025 wave of math-task RL specializers.

papers.title: Open-Reasoner-Zero: An Open Source Approach to Scaling Up Reinforcement Learning on the Base Model
papers.authors: Jingcheng Hu, Yinmin Zhang, Qi Han, Daxin Jiang, Xiangyu Zhang, Heung-Yeung Shum
papers.inclusion_reason: Open reproduction of R1-Zero-style math RL: vanilla PPO with rule-based rewards and no KL regularization specializes Qwen2.5-32B base on AIME/MATH-500, matching DeepSeek-R1-Zero at 1/10 the steps. Explicitly a math-task specializer with full open code/data.

papers.title: SimpleRL-Zoo: Investigating and Taming Zero Reinforcement Learning for Open Base Models in the Wild
papers.authors: Weihao Zeng, Yuzhen Huang, Qian Liu, Wei Liu, Keqing He, Zejun Ma, Junxian He
papers.inclusion_reason: SimpleRL-Zero cookbook: applies zero RL (rule-based math rewards) across 10 open base models to specialize them on math reasoning, cataloging what design choices (format reward, difficulty control) make math-task RL work. Directly targets math as the specialization objective.

papers.title: ReFT: Reasoning with Reinforced Fine-Tuning
papers.authors: Trung Quoc Luong, Xinbo Zhang, Zhanming Jie, Peng Sun, Xiaoran Jin, Hang Li
papers.inclusion_reason: Early, influential RL-for-math specializer: SFT warmup then online PPO with rewards derived from ground-truth answers, evaluated on GSM8K/MathQA/SVAMP. Demonstrates RL improving math generalization over SFT from the same questions; a pre-R1 template for the subarea.

papers.title: VinePPO: Unlocking RL Potential For LLM Reasoning Through Refined Credit Assignment
papers.authors: Amirhossein Kazemnejad, Milad Aghajohari, Eva Portelance, Alessandro Sordoni, Siva Reddy, Aaron Courville, Nicolas Le Roux
papers.inclusion_reason: Credit-assignment method for math RL: replaces PPO value networks with Monte-Carlo advantage estimates to specialize models on MATH/GSM8K, outperforming PPO in far fewer steps. Central to the methodological discussion of how to assign per-step credit in math-task RL.

papers.title: rStar-Math: Small LLMs Can Master Math Reasoning with Self-Evolved Deep Thinking
papers.authors: Xinyu Guan, Li Lyna Zhang, Yifei Liu, Ning Shang, Youran Sun, Yi Zhu, Fan Yang, Mao Yang
papers.inclusion_reason: Self-evolution RL for math specialization: iteratively co-trains a policy SLM and a process preference model via MCTS rollouts, driving a 7B model to 53.3% on AIME. A leading example of process-reward + search-based RL specialization of small models on math.

papers.title: Qwen2.5-Math Technical Report: Toward Mathematical Expert Model via Self-Improvement
papers.authors: An Yang, Beichen Zhang, Binyuan Hui, Bofei Gao, Bowen Yu, Chengpeng Li, Dayiheng Liu, Jianhong Tu, Jingren Zhou, Junyang Lin, et al.
papers.inclusion_reason: Production math-specialist model built with RL: develops a math reward model from massive sampling and applies RL (GRPO-family) as the final stage to produce Qwen2.5-Math-Instruct. Widely used as the base for later math-RL work; a concrete end-to-end math-task specialization pipeline.
papers.taxonomy_node: reward/learned reward model; optim/group policy gradient

papers.title: ReST-MCTS*: LLM Self-Training via Process Reward Guided Tree Search
papers.authors: Dan Zhang, Sining Zhoubian, Ziniu Hu, Yisong Yue, Yuxiao Dong, Jie Tang
papers.inclusion_reason: Process-reward + tree-search self-training for math/reasoning: infers per-step process rewards from oracle answers via MCTS and uses the searched traces to iteratively reinforce policy and reward models on math benchmarks. Bridges process-reward RL and search-based specialization.

papers.title: DeepSeek-Prover-V1.5: Harnessing Proof Assistant Feedback for Reinforcement Learning and Monte-Carlo Tree Search
papers.authors: Huajian Xin, Z. Z. Ren, Junxiao Song, Zhihong Shao, Wanjia Zhao, Haocheng Wang, Bo Liu, Liyue Zhang, Xuan Lu, Qiushi Du, et al.
papers.inclusion_reason: RL for theorem proving (formal math specialization): reinforcement learning from proof assistant feedback (RLPAF) plus RMaxTS Monte-Carlo tree search specializes a model for Lean 4 proving, setting SOTA on miniF2F/ProofNet. Extends task-specific math RL to the formal-verification reward setting.

papers.title: Seq2SQL: Generating Structured Queries from Natural Language using Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Victor Zhong, Caiming Xiong, Richard Socher
papers.inclusion_reason: Seminal work specializing a seq2seq model on text-to-SQL via policy-based RL with in-the-loop query execution as reward, exploiting SQL structure that cross-entropy handles poorly. The archetypal execution-accuracy-reward RL task specialization; released WikiSQL.

papers.title: SQL-R1: Training Natural Language to SQL Reasoning Model By Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Peixian Ma, Xialie Zhuang, Chengjin Xu, Xuhui Jiang, Ran Chen, Jian Guo
papers.inclusion_reason: Modern RL specialization of an LLM for text-to-SQL reasoning with a task-tailored reward (execution correctness plus format/length constraints); reaches 88.6% on Spider and 67.1% on BIRD. Canonical recent execution-guided RL-for-SQL result.

papers.title: Arctic-Text2SQL-R1: Simple Rewards, Strong Reasoning in Text-to-SQL
papers.authors: Zhewei Yao, Guoheng Sun, Lukasz Borchmann, Gaurav Nuti, Zheyu Shen, Minghang Deng, Bohan Zhai, Hao Zhang, Ang Li, Yuxiong He
papers.inclusion_reason: Shows a simple execution-correctness reward is enough to RL-specialize an LLM into a strong text-to-SQL reasoner, arguing against complex reward shaping. Directly informs the reward-design axis of task-specific RL for SQL.

papers.title: Reasoning-SQL: Reinforcement Learning with SQL Tailored Partial Rewards for Reasoning-Enhanced Text-to-SQL
papers.authors: Mohammadreza Pourreza, Shayan Talaei, Ruoxi Sun, Xingchen Wan, Hailong Li, Azalia Mirhoseini, Amin Saberi, Sercan O. Arik
papers.inclusion_reason: Attacks the sparse-binary-execution-reward problem with SQL-tailored partial rewards (schema-linking, AI feedback, n-gram similarity, syntax check) under GRPO; RL-only beats SFT for generalization. Central to task-specific reward engineering for SQL.

papers.title: Reward-SQL: Boosting Text-to-SQL via Stepwise Execution-Aware Reasoning and Process-Supervised Rewards
papers.authors: Yuxin Zhang, Meihao Fan, Ju Fan, Mingyang Yi, Yuyu Luo, Guoliang Li, Bin Wu, Wenchao Zhou
papers.inclusion_reason: Introduces process-supervised (stepwise, execution-aware) rewards rather than only outcome execution accuracy for text-to-SQL, a distinct point on the reward-granularity spectrum for RL task specialization.

papers.title: Graph-Reward-SQL: Execution-Free Reinforcement Learning for Text-to-SQL via Graph Matching and Stepwise Reward
papers.authors: Han Weng, Puzhen Wu, Longjie Cui, Yi Zhan, Boyi Liu, Yuanfeng Song, Dun Zeng, Yingxiang Yang, Qianru Zhang, Dong Huang, Xiaoming Yin, Yang Sun, Xing Chen
papers.inclusion_reason: Replaces costly execution-based reward with an execution-free graph-matching stepwise reward for RL text-to-SQL specialization, addressing the practical bottleneck of running queries against a live DB every rollout.
papers.taxonomy_node: reward/learned reward model; optim/group policy gradient

papers.title: Learning to summarize from human feedback
papers.authors: Nisan Stiennon, Long Ouyang, Jeff Wu, Daniel M. Ziegler, Ryan Lowe, Chelsea Voss, Alec Radford, Dario Amodei, Paul Christiano
papers.inclusion_reason: Seminal task-specific RL specialization of an LLM on summarization: fit a reward model from human comparisons on one task, then PPO against it, beating supervised/ROUGE-optimized baselines. The template for RL specialization with a task-specific learned reward.

papers.title: Learning to Generate Structured Output with Schema Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Yaxi Lu, Haolun Li, Xin Cong, Zhong Zhang, Yesai Wu, Yankai Lin, Zhiyuan Liu, Fangming Liu, Maosong Sun
papers.inclusion_reason: RL-specializes an LLM to emit schema-valid JSON using a fine-grained schema validator as reward; introduces SchemaBench (~40K schemas). Core example of structured-output generation as an RL-with-verifiable-reward task.

papers.title: Think Inside the JSON: Reinforcement Strategy for Strict LLM Schema Adherence
papers.authors: Bhavik Agarwal, Ishan Joshi, Viktoria Rojkova
papers.inclusion_reason: Trains a 1.5B model with GRPO and custom rewards checking JSON validity, structural faithfulness, and tag correctness to enforce strict schema adherence. Concrete task-specific reward design for constrained structured generation.

papers.title: A Reasoning Paradigm for Named Entity Recognition
papers.authors: Hui Huang, Yanping Chen, Ruizhang Huang, Chuan Lin, Yongbin Qin
papers.inclusion_reason: Shifts NER from implicit pattern matching to explicit reasoning, enhancing the reasoning process with RL (GRPO-style verifiable reward, keeping only verifiably-correct reasoning paths) to specialize an LLM on the extraction task; reported SOTA zero-shot NER.

papers.title: Reinforcement Learning for Relation Classification from Noisy Data
papers.authors: Jun Feng, Minlie Huang, Li Zhao, Yang Yang, Xiaoyan Zhu
papers.inclusion_reason: Task-specific RL for relation extraction: an RL instance selector picks high-quality sentences, rewarded by the relation classifier, to denoise distantly-supervised data. Early information-extraction specialization via RL reward, predating the LLM era.

papers.title: Relation Mention Extraction from Noisy Data with Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Jun Feng, Minlie Huang, Yijie Zhang, Yang Yang, Xiaoyan Zhu
papers.inclusion_reason: Hierarchical RL for relation mention extraction: a top-level sentence selector plus low-level mention extractor with a reward estimator, denoising and extracting without explicit annotations. Complements the IE/relation-extraction RL axis of the survey.

papers.title: HuatuoGPT-o1, Towards Medical Complex Reasoning with LLMs
papers.authors: Junying Chen, Zhenyang Cai, Ke Ji, Xidong Wang, Wanlong Liu, Rongsheng Wang, Jianye Hou, Benyou Wang
papers.inclusion_reason: Seminal domain-vertical task RL: constructs 40K verifiable medical problems and uses a medical verifier as an RL reward (after SFT) to specialize an LLM on medical complex reasoning, the canonical medical RLVR reference.

papers.title: Med-R1: Reinforcement Learning for Generalizable Medical Reasoning in Vision-Language Models
papers.authors: Yuxiang Lai, Jike Zhong, Ming Li, Shitian Zhao, Yuheng Li, Konstantinos Psounis, Xiaofeng Yang
papers.inclusion_reason: Applies GRPO with verifiable rewards to specialize a medical vision-language model across eight imaging modalities; a 2B model gains ~30% accuracy and beats a 72B baseline, showing RL for task-specific medical multimodal specialization.

papers.title: MT-R1-Zero: Advancing LLM-based Machine Translation via R1-Zero-like Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Zhaopeng Feng, Shaosheng Cao, Jiahan Ren, Jiayuan Su, Ruizhe Chen, Yan Zhang, Zhe Xu, Yao Hu, Jian Wu, Zuozhu Liu
papers.inclusion_reason: R1-Zero-style RL specializing an LLM on the machine-translation task, using metric-based (COMET/BLEU) verifiable rewards; a clean example of task-specific reward design for a non-math vertical.

papers.title: SSR-Zero: Simple Self-Rewarding Reinforcement Learning for Machine Translation
papers.authors: Wenjie Yang, Mao Zheng, Mingyang Song, Zheng Li, Sitong Wang
papers.inclusion_reason: Task-specific MT RL with a self-rewarding signal (no external reward model or references); trained on 13K monolingual examples, a 7B model beats a 32B instruct baseline on EN-ZH, illustrating reward-design variety in the translation vertical.

papers.title: Legal-Delta: Enhancing Legal Reasoning in LLMs via Reinforcement Learning with Chain-of-Thought Guided Information Gain
papers.authors: Xin Dai, Buqiang Xu, Zhenghao Liu, Yukun Yan, Huiyuan Xie, Xiaoyuan Yi, Shuo Wang, Ge Yu
papers.inclusion_reason: RL framework specializing an LLM on the legal-reasoning task, using a chain-of-thought guided information-gain reward; represents the legal vertical of task-specific RL.

papers.title: Fin-R1: A Large Language Model for Financial Reasoning through Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Zhaowei Liu, Xin Guo, Zhi Yang, Fangqi Lou, Lingfeng Zeng, Jinyi Niu, Mengping Li, Qi Qi, Zhiqiang Liu, Yiyang Han, Dongpo Cheng, Ronghao Chen, Huacan Wang, Xingdong Feng, Huixia Judy Wang, Chengchun Shi, Liwen Zhang
papers.inclusion_reason: Two-stage SFT-then-RL pipeline specializing a 7B model on financial reasoning; SOTA on FinQA/ConvFinQA and shows RL beats continued pretraining, the canonical finance-vertical task RL reference.

papers.title: Trading-R1: Financial Trading with LLM Reasoning via Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Yijia Xiao, Edward Sun, Tong Chen, Fang Wu, Di Luo, Wei Wang
papers.inclusion_reason: Specializes an LLM on the financial-trading task via RL over 100K financial records, with reward tied to structured, evidence-based investment theses; a distinct finance-vertical task (trading decisions vs. QA).

papers.title: Visual-RFT: Visual Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
papers.authors: Ziyu Liu, Zeyi Sun, Yuhang Zang, Xiaoyi Dong, Yuhang Cao, Haodong Duan, Dahua Lin, Jiaqi Wang
papers.inclusion_reason: Seminal multimodal task RL: defines visual-perception verifiable reward functions and uses GRPO to specialize a vision-language model on tasks like detection and fine-grained classification with scarce data.

papers.title: VLM-R1: A Stable and Generalizable R1-style Large Vision-Language Model
papers.authors: Haozhan Shen, Peng Liu, Jingcheng Li, Chunxin Fang, Yibo Ma, Jiajia Liao, Qiaoli Shen, Zilun Zhang, Kangjia Zhao, Qianqian Zhang, Ruochen Xu, Tiancheng Zhao
papers.inclusion_reason: R1-style rule-based-reward RL specializing a VLM on referring-expression comprehension and open-vocabulary detection; analyzes reward hacking and generalization vs. SFT, a core multimodal-vertical task RL reference.

papers.title: Reason-RFT: Reinforcement Fine-Tuning for Visual Reasoning of Vision Language Models
papers.authors: Huajie Tan, Yuheng Ji, Xiaoshuai Hao, Xiansheng Chen, Pengwei Wang, Zhongyuan Wang, Shanghang Zhang
papers.inclusion_reason: SFT-then-GRPO framework specializing VLMs on visual-reasoning tasks (counting, structural perception, spatial transformation) with verifiable rewards; emphasizes out-of-domain generalization, extending the multimodal vertical.

papers.title: MolReasoner: Toward Effective and Interpretable Reasoning for Molecular LLMs
papers.authors: Guojiang Zhao, Zixiang Lu, Yutang Ge, Sihang Li, Zheng Cheng, Haitao Lin, Lirong Wu, Hanchen Xia, Hengxing Cai, Wentao Guo, Hongshuai Wang, Mingjun Xu, Siyu Zhu, Guolin Ke, Linfeng Zhang, Zhifeng Gao
papers.inclusion_reason: Specializes an LLM on molecular tasks (molecule captioning and generation) via SFT then GRPO with chemistry-specific verifiable rewards (SMILES/fingerprint structural similarity); the chemistry/scientific vertical.
papers.taxonomy_node: reward/learned reward model; optim/group policy gradient

papers.title: CodeRL+: Improving Code Generation via Reinforcement with Execution Semantics Alignment
papers.authors: Xue Jiang, Yihong Dong, Mengyang Liu, Hongyi Deng, Tian Wang, Yongding Tao, Rongyu Cao, Binhua Li, Zhi Jin, Wenpin Jiao, Fei Huang, Yongbin Li, Ge Li
papers.inclusion_reason: Extends execution-based RL for code by explicitly aligning the policy with program execution semantics (runtime behavior) rather than only pass/fail, bridging text-based synthesis and functional correctness. Recent refinement of the execution-grounded reward mechanism.

papers.title: Self-Rewarding Language Models
papers.authors: Weizhe Yuan, Richard Yuanzhe Pang, Kyunghyun Cho, Xian Li, Sainbayar Sukhbaatar, Jing Xu, Jason Weston
papers.inclusion_reason: Foundational: the model uses LLM-as-a-Judge prompting to reward its own generations during iterative DPO, the canonical self-generated judge reward for RL fine-tuning.

papers.title: Generative Verifiers: Reward Modeling as Next-Token Prediction
papers.authors: Lunjun Zhang, Arian Hosseini, Hritik Bansal, Mehran Kazemi, Aviral Kumar, Rishabh Agarwal
papers.inclusion_reason: Seminal GenRM formulation that trains verifiers via next-token prediction, giving a generative reward signal that outperforms discriminative verifiers and LLM-as-a-Judge on reasoning tasks.

papers.title: Generative Reward Models
papers.authors: Dakota Mahan, Duy Van Phung, Rafael Rafailov, Chase Blagden, Nathan Lile, Louis Castricato, Jan-Philipp Franken, Chelsea Finn, Alon Albalak
papers.inclusion_reason: Introduces CoT-GenRM unifying RLHF and RLAIF, training an LLM judge on self-generated reasoning to produce preference rewards that beat LLM-as-a-judge in- and out-of-distribution.

papers.title: Inference-Time Scaling for Generalist Reward Modeling
papers.authors: Zijun Liu, Peiyi Wang, Runxin Xu, Shirong Ma, Chong Ruan, Peng Li, Yang Liu, Yu Wu
papers.inclusion_reason: DeepSeek-GRM: pointwise generative reward model trained with online RL (Self-Principled Critique Tuning) to give a scalable judge reward for tasks lacking a hard verifier.

papers.title: Critique-out-Loud Reward Models
papers.authors: Zachary Ankner, Mansheej Paul, Brandon Cui, Jonathan D. Chang, Prithviraj Ammanabrolu
papers.inclusion_reason: CLoud reward models generate a natural-language critique before predicting a scalar reward, a reference generative-reward design used to score RL/Best-of-N candidates.

papers.title: J1: Incentivizing Thinking in LLM-as-a-Judge via Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Chenxi Whitehouse, Tianlu Wang, Ping Yu, Xian Li, Jason Weston, Ilia Kulikov, Swarnadeep Saha
papers.inclusion_reason: RL-trains an LLM-as-a-Judge to reason before verdicts, converting both verifiable and non-verifiable prompts into a unified verifiable-reward format for judge optimization.

papers.title: Think-J: Learning to Think for Generative LLM-as-a-Judge
papers.authors: Hui Huang, Yancheng He, Hongli Zhou, Rui Zhang, Wei Liu, Weixun Wang, Jiaheng Liu, Wenbo Su
papers.inclusion_reason: Trains a generative LLM-as-a-judge to think via RL over judgment reasoning, improving the reliability of judge-based rewards over generative and classifier baselines.

papers.title: Self-Taught Evaluators
papers.authors: Tianlu Wang, Ilia Kulikov, Olga Golovneva, Ping Yu, Weizhe Yuan, Jane Dwivedi-Yu, Richard Yuanzhe Pang, Maryam Fazel-Zarandi, Jason Weston, Xian Li
papers.inclusion_reason: Builds a strong LLM-as-a-Judge from synthetic contrasting preference pairs with no human labels, supplying a reference-free judge reward source for downstream RL.

papers.title: Crossing the Reward Bridge: Expanding RL with Verifiable Rewards Across Diverse Domains
papers.authors: Yi Su, Dian Yu, Linfeng Song, Juntao Li, Haitao Mi, Zhaopeng Tu, Min Zhang, Dong Yu
papers.inclusion_reason: Distills compact generative (model-based) verifiers to give soft judge rewards for free-form, multi-domain answers where rule-based verification fails, driving RL gains.

papers.title: RLPR: Extrapolating RLVR to General Domains without Verifiers
papers.authors: Tianyu Yu, Bo Ji, Shouli Wang, Shu Yao, Zefan Wang, Ganqu Cui, Lifan Yuan, Ning Ding, Yuan Yao, Zhiyuan Liu, Maosong Sun, Tat-Seng Chua
papers.inclusion_reason: Verifier-free RL that uses the LLM's own token-probability score for a reference answer as a reference-based generative reward, extending RLVR to tasks without a hard verifier.

papers.title: One Token to Fool LLM-as-a-Judge
papers.authors: Yulai Zhao, Haolin Liu, Dian Yu, S. Y. Kung, Haitao Mi, Dong Yu
papers.inclusion_reason: Reliability/bias evidence: generative reward models are systematically reward-hackable by 'master key' tokens, giving false-positive rewards that threaten judge-based RL signals.

papers.title: LLM-as-a-Judge & Reward Model: What They Can and Cannot Do
papers.authors: Guijin Son, Hyunwoo Ko, Hoyoung Lee, Yewon Kim, Seunghyeok Hong
papers.inclusion_reason: Reliability/bias analysis of what LLM judges and reward models can and cannot reliably evaluate, informing when a generative judge is a trustworthy RL reward.

papers.title: Rubrics as Rewards: Reinforcement Learning Beyond Verifiable Domains
papers.authors: Anisha Gunjal, Anthony Wang, Elaine Lau, Vaskar Nath, Yunzhong He, Bing Liu, Sean Hendryx
papers.inclusion_reason: Seminal 'Rubrics as Rewards' (RaR) framework: on-policy RL (GRPO) that uses instance-specific checklist rubrics as the reward for specializing an LLM to non-verifiable tasks (medicine, science), where no hard verifier exists. Central to the rubric-as-reward subarea.
papers.taxonomy_node: reward/learned reward model; optim/group policy gradient

papers.title: Checklists Are Better Than Reward Models For Aligning Language Models
papers.authors: Vijay Viswanathan, Yanchao Sun, Shuang Ma, Xiang Kong, Meng Cao, Graham Neubig, Tongshuang Wu
papers.inclusion_reason: Proposes Reinforcement Learning from Checklist Feedback (RLCF): extracts instruction-specific checklists and scores responses per item via AI judges and verifier programs to form the RL reward, directly contrasting checklist/rubric rewards with trained reward models for task-specific instruction following.
papers.taxonomy_node: reward/learned reward model; optim/group policy gradient

papers.title: OpenRubrics: Towards Scalable Synthetic Rubric Generation for Reward Modeling and LLM Alignment
papers.authors: Tianci Liu, Ran Xu, Tony Yu, Ilgee Hong, Carl Yang, Tuo Zhao, Haoyu Wang
papers.inclusion_reason: Scalable synthetic rubric generation (Contrastive Rubric Generation) to train rubric-based reward models; shows rubric-derived rewards transfer to policy models in instruction-following and biomedical tasks. Bridges learned reward models and rubric-as-reward for domains lacking hard verifiers.
papers.taxonomy_node: reward/learned reward model; optim/group policy gradient

papers.title: Fine-Grained Human Feedback Gives Better Rewards for Language Model Training
papers.authors: Zeqiu Wu, Yushi Hu, Weijia Shi, Nouha Dziri, Alane Suhr, Prithviraj Ammanabrolu, Noah A. Smith, Mari Ostendorf, Hannaneh Hajishirzi
papers.inclusion_reason: Seminal fine-grained/aspect reward-model work: trains multiple aspect-specific reward models (factuality, relevance, completeness) giving dense per-segment rewards, used to customize LM behavior on detoxification and long-form QA. Foundational for aspect/multi-objective task-specific learned rewards.

papers.title: Scaling Laws for Reward Model Overoptimization
papers.authors: Leo Gao, John Schulman, Jacob Hilton
papers.inclusion_reason: Seminal, most-cited study of reward hacking / overoptimization of a learned proxy reward model: quantifies how RL against an imperfect learned reward diverges from true reward. Essential for the reward-hacking-of-learned-task-rewards angle.

papers.title: Scaling Laws for Reward Model Overoptimization in Direct Alignment Algorithms
papers.authors: Rafael Rafailov, Yaswanth Chittepu, Ryan Park, Harshit Sikchi, Joey Hejna, Bradley Knox, Chelsea Finn, Scott Niekum
papers.inclusion_reason: Extends reward-model overoptimization / hacking analysis to direct alignment algorithms (DPO-style), showing implicit reward overoptimization even without an explicit trained reward model. Key for understanding reward hacking of learned task rewards.

papers.title: Reward Hacking in the Era of Large Models: Mechanisms, Emergent Misalignment, Challenges
papers.authors: Xiaohua Wang, Muzhao Tian, Yuqi Zeng, Zisu Huang, Jiakang Yuan, Bowen Chen, Jingwen Xu, Mingbo Zhou, Wenhao Liu, Muling Wu, Zhengkang Guo, Qi Qian, Yifei Wang, Feiran Zhang, Ruicheng Yin, Shihan Dou, Changze Lv, Tao Chen, Kaitao Song, Xu Tan, Tao Gui, Xiaoqing Zheng, Xuanjing Huang
papers.inclusion_reason: Recent survey/analysis of reward-hacking mechanisms across learned reward models in large-model RL, covering OOD robustness and reward misspecification. Provides the modern framing of reward hacking of learned task-specific rewards.

papers.title: Reward Hacking in Rubric-Based Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Anas Mahmoud, MohammadHossein Rezaei, Zihao Wang, Anisha Gunjal, Bing Liu, Yunzhong He
papers.inclusion_reason: Directly studies reward hacking specific to rubric-based RL rewards (LLM-judge biases exploited during rubric-scored RL). Exactly on the intersection of rubric-as-reward and reward hacking called for in this subarea.

papers.title: RUBRIC-ARROW: Alternating Pointwise Rubric Reward Modeling for LLM Post-training in Non-verifiable Domains
papers.authors: Haoxiang Jiang, Zihan Dong, Tianci Liu, Wanying Wang, Ran Xu, Tony Yu, Linjun Zhang, Haoyu Wang
papers.inclusion_reason: Jointly trains a rubric generator and a rubric-conditioned judge to produce pointwise rubric rewards for LLM post-training in non-verifiable domains. Recent instantiation of learned rubric reward modeling for task specialization.

papers.title: Many Voices, One Reward: Multi-Role Rubric Generation for LLM Judging and Reward Modeling
papers.authors: Dazhi Fu, Jiuding Yang, Yiwen Guo, Jicong Fan
papers.inclusion_reason: Multi-role rubric generation to build rubric-based rewards/judges, improving reward-model quality for subjective tasks. Recent rubric-as-reward-modeling method relevant to task-specific reward design without hard verifiers.

papers.title: Rewarding Progress: Scaling Automated Process Verifiers for LLM Reasoning
papers.authors: Amrith Setlur, Chirag Nagpal, Adam Fisch, Xinyang Geng, Jacob Eisenstein, Rishabh Agarwal, Alekh Agarwal, Jonathan Berant, Aviral Kumar
papers.inclusion_reason: Formalizes process reward as measuring progress and scales automated process verifiers used to shape RL for a specific task (reasoning). Advances design of learned dense process rewards for task-specific RL.

papers.title: The Lessons of Developing Process Reward Models in Mathematical Reasoning
papers.authors: Zhenru Zhang, Chujie Zheng, Yangzhen Wu, Beichen Zhang, Runji Lin, Bowen Yu, Dayiheng Liu, Jingren Zhou, Junyang Lin
papers.inclusion_reason: Detailed empirical study (Qwen team) of how to train task-specific process reward models for math reasoning, exposing data-synthesis pitfalls and reward-model design lessons. Practical guidance for building learned reward models to shape a specific task.

papers.title: Understanding R1-Zero-Like Training: A Critical Perspective
papers.authors: Zichen Liu, Changyu Chen, Wenjun Li, Penghui Qi, Tianyu Pang, Chao Du, Wee Sun Lee, Min Lin
papers.inclusion_reason: Introduces Dr. GRPO, an unbiased GRPO variant that removes GRPO's length/difficulty optimization bias, and critically analyzes base-model and RL choices in R1-Zero-style training. Central algorithm/hyperparameter study for task RFT.

papers.title: Group Sequence Policy Optimization
papers.authors: Chujie Zheng, Shixuan Liu, Mingze Li, Xiong-Hui Chen, Bowen Yu, et al. (Qwen Team, Alibaba)
papers.inclusion_reason: Introduces GSPO, a sequence-level (rather than token-level) importance-ratio GRPO variant used to train the Qwen3 models; stabilizes MoE RL and simplifies RL infrastructure. A recent, influential recipe/algorithm for task RL.

papers.title: Tulu 3: Pushing Frontiers in Open Language Model Post-Training
papers.authors: Nathan Lambert, Jacob Morrison, Valentina Pyatkin, Shengyi Huang, Hamish Ivison, et al. (Allen Institute for AI)
papers.inclusion_reason: A fully open post-training recipe that popularizes Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) for task-specific skills, with released data, code, and detailed training recipe. Cornerstone open-recipe reference.

papers.title: Reinforcement Learning for Reasoning in Large Language Models with One Training Example
papers.authors: Yiping Wang, Qing Yang, Zhiyuan Zeng, Liliang Ren, Lucas Liu, Baolin Peng, Hao Cheng, Xuehai He, Kuan Wang, Jianfeng Gao, Weizhu Chen, Shuohang Wang, Simon Shaolei Du, Yelong Shen
papers.inclusion_reason: Landmark data-efficiency result: 1-shot RLVR with a single example dramatically boosts math reasoning across GRPO and PPO. Central to the sample-efficient / small-data RFT theme.

papers.title: OpenRLHF: An Easy-to-use, Scalable and High-performance RLHF Framework
papers.authors: Jian Hu, Xibin Wu, Zilin Zhu, Xianyu, Weixun Wang, Dehao Zhang, Yu Cao
papers.inclusion_reason: A widely-used open-source RLHF/RL training framework (Ray + vLLM + DeepSpeed) supporting PPO, GRPO, REINFORCE++ and more. Key infrastructure enabling task-specific RFT at scale.

papers.title: HybridFlow: A Flexible and Efficient RLHF Framework
papers.authors: Guangming Sheng, Chi Zhang, Zilingfeng Ye, Xibin Wu, Wang Zhang, Ru Zhang, Yanghua Peng, Haibin Lin, Chuan Wu
papers.inclusion_reason: The paper behind verl, the hybrid single/multi-controller RL post-training framework on which many task-RFT recipes (e.g. DAPO) are built. Foundational open-source RL training framework.

papers.title: ProRL: Prolonged Reinforcement Learning Expands Reasoning Boundaries in Large Language Models
papers.authors: Mingjie Liu, Shizhe Diao, Ximing Lu, Jian Hu, Xin Dong, Yejin Choi, Jan Kautz, Yi Dong (NVIDIA)
papers.inclusion_reason: A prolonged-training RL recipe with KL-regularized trust regions, reference-policy resets, and length penalties; shows extended RL genuinely expands reasoning. Key practical recipe/hyperparameter study for stable long-run task RFT.

papers.title: VAPO: Efficient and Reliable Reinforcement Learning for Advanced Reasoning Tasks
papers.authors: Yu Yue, Yufeng Yuan, Qiying Yu, Xiaochen Zuo, Ruofei Zhu, et al. (ByteDance Seed)
papers.inclusion_reason: A value-based (augmented PPO) recipe addressing value-model bias, heterogeneous sequence lengths, and reward sparsity for long-CoT RL; SOTA on AIME 2024 over DAPO. Important counterpoint recipe to critic-free GRPO variants.

papers.title: Does Reinforcement Learning Really Incentivize Reasoning Capacity in LLMs Beyond the Base Model?
papers.authors: Yang Yue, Zhiqi Chen, Rui Lu, Andrew Zhao, Zhaokai Wang, Yang Yue, Shiji Song, Gao Huang
papers.inclusion_reason: The central 'does RLVR elicit vs teach new capability' paper: shows RLVR-trained models beat base at pass@1 but base wins at large pass@k, arguing RL sharpens the base model's existing distribution rather than creating new reasoning. Foundational to the science of what task RL actually does.

papers.title: SFT Memorizes, RL Generalizes: A Comparative Study of Foundation Model Post-training
papers.authors: Tianzhe Chu, Yuexiang Zhai, Jihan Yang, Shengbang Tong, Saining Xie, Dale Schuurmans, Quoc V. Le, Sergey Levine, Yi Ma
papers.inclusion_reason: The canonical 'SFT memorizes, RL generalizes' study: outcome-reward RL generalizes across rule/visual variants (GeneralPoints, V-IRL) while SFT overfits, yet SFT is still needed to stabilize format for RL. Directly frames the RL-vs-SFT-for-a-task question central to this subarea.

papers.title: Spurious Rewards: Rethinking Training Signals in RLVR
papers.authors: Rulin Shao, Shuyue Stella Li, Rui Xin, Scott Geng, Yiping Wang, Sewoong Oh, Simon Shaolei Du, Nathan Lambert, Sewon Min, Ranjay Krishna, Yulia Tsvetkov, Hannaneh Hajishirzi, Pang Wei Koh, Luke Zettlemoyer
papers.inclusion_reason: Shows RLVR with random/spurious rewards still lifts Qwen2.5-Math by ~21pts (near ground-truth gains) via clipping bias amplifying pre-existing behaviors, but fails on Llama/OLMo. Sharpest evidence that task RL often elicits latent capability rather than teaching, and that this is model-family-dependent.

papers.title: RL's Razor: Why Online Reinforcement Learning Forgets Less
papers.authors: Idan Shenfeld, Jyothish Pari, Pulkit Agrawal
papers.inclusion_reason: Explains why RL-specialized models forget prior abilities less than SFT: on-policy RL is implicitly biased toward KL-minimal solutions on the new task. Core mechanistic account of catastrophic forgetting under task RL vs SFT.

papers.title: The Entropy Mechanism of Reinforcement Learning for Reasoning Language Models
papers.authors: Ganqu Cui, Yuchen Zhang, Jiacheng Chen, Lifan Yuan, Zhi Wang, Yuxin Zuo, Haozhan Li, Yuchen Fan, Huayu Chen, Weize Chen, Zhiyuan Liu, Hao Peng, Lei Bai, Wanli Ouyang, Yu Cheng, Bowen Zhou, Ning Ding
papers.inclusion_reason: Establishes the entropy-collapse dynamics of task RL: policy entropy drops sharply early and its exhaustion caps performance, with an empirical R = -a*exp(H)+b law linking entropy to reward. The reference account of diversity loss / entropy collapse during specialization.

papers.title: The Choice of Divergence: A Neglected Key to Mitigating Diversity Collapse in Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Reward
papers.authors: Long Li, Zhijian Zhou, Jiaran Hao, Jason Klein Liu, Yanting Miao, Wei Pang, Xiaoyu Tan, Wei Chu, Zhe Wang, Shirui Pan, Chao Qu, Yuan Qi
papers.inclusion_reason: Diagnoses the specialization paradox where pass@1 improves while pass@k degrades (diversity collapse), tying it to divergence choice and proposing mass-covering divergences (DPH-RL) to preserve solution diversity. Directly characterizes the diversity-loss cost of task RL.

papers.title: Can RL Improve Generalization of LLM Agents? An Empirical Study
papers.authors: Zhiheng Xi, Xin Guo, Jiaqi Liu, Jiazheng Zhang, Yutao Fan, Zhihao Zhang, Shichun Liu, Mingxu Chai, Xiaowei Shi, Yitao Zhai, Xunliang Cai, Tao Gui, Qi Zhang, Xuanjing Huang
papers.inclusion_reason: Empirical study of out-of-distribution generalization for RL-specialized agents: RFT generalizes across task difficulty within an environment but transfers weakly to unseen environments, and quantifies transfer vs forgetting under sequential multi-environment training. Directly on OOD generalization of task RL.

papers.title: Revisiting Catastrophic Forgetting in Large Language Model Tuning
papers.authors: Hongyu Li, Liang Ding, Meng Fang, Dacheng Tao
papers.inclusion_reason: Connects catastrophic forgetting severity to loss-landscape sharpness during fine-tuning and shows sharpness-aware minimization reduces it across scales. Grounds the loss-of-general-ability dynamics that task-specific tuning induces.

papers.title: Mapping Post-Training Forgetting in Language Models at Scale
papers.authors: Jackson Harmon, Andreas Hochlehnert, Matthias Bethge, Ameya Prabhu
papers.inclusion_reason: A scalable framework measuring knowledge loss during post-training via sample-wise correct->incorrect transitions, mapping how much SFT and RL (GRPO) forget across sizes and methods. Empirical measurement backbone for forgetting under task specialization.

papers.title: The Climb Carves Wisdom Deeper Than the Summit: On the Noisy Rewards in Learning to Reason
papers.authors: Ang Lv, Ruobing Xie, Xingwu Sun, Zhanhui Kang, Rui Yan
papers.inclusion_reason: Quantifies RLVR robustness to noisy verifiable rewards: flipping 40% of rewards still yields strong task gains, and rewarding reasoning-pattern presence rivals strict correctness verification.

papers.title: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards Implicitly Incentivizes Correct Reasoning in Base LLMs
papers.authors: Xumeng Wen, Zihan Liu, Shun Zheng, Shengyu Ye, Zhirong Wu, Yang Wang, Zhijian Xu, Xiao Liang, Junjie Li, Ziming Miao, Jiang Bian, Mao Yang
papers.inclusion_reason: Counterpoint to the pass@k critique: shows via CoT-Pass@K that verifiable rewards do extend the reasoning boundary on math and code, not just reweight existing traces.

papers.title: Reasoning or Memorization? Unreliable Results of Reinforcement Learning Due to Data Contamination
papers.authors: Mingqi Wu, Zhihao Zhang, Qiaole Dong, Zhiheng Xi, Jun Zhao, Senjie Jin, Xiaoran Fan, Yuhao Zhou, Huijie Lv, Ming Zhang, Yanwei Fu, Qin Liu, Songyang Zhang, Qi Zhang
papers.inclusion_reason: Argues spurious/random-reward RLVR gains are largely a data-contamination retrieval artifact, a key caveat for trusting verifiable-reward task specialization results.

papers.title: Fin-o1: On the Transferability of Reasoning-Enhanced LLMs and Reinforcement Learning to Finance
papers.authors: Lingfei Qian, Weipeng Zhou, Yan Wang, Xueqing Peng, Han Yi, Yilun Zhao, Jimin Huang, Qianqian Xie, Jian-yun Nie
papers.inclusion_reason: Studies transferability of reasoning-enhancement and RL to the finance task, building a financial-reasoning specialist and probing when task-specific RL helps a vertical; complements Fin-R1 in the finance vertical.
